title: Rice Wine Pilaf on Sunday
description: A smoky and crispy Algerian dish featuring rice wine in a simple, egg-free pilaf recipe that's perfect for lazy Sundays.

ingredients.name: Rice Wine (optional)
ingredients.notes: To add smokiness and depth of flavor, can be omitted if desired

instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C). Rinse and drain the basmati rice.

Season chicken pieces with salt and pepper. If using, add a bit of olive oil and a small amount of rice wine to the pan for extra smokiness; otherwise, just cook them in the pan with a little olive oil until golden brown on all sides.
In a separate skillet, sauté onions and garlic until translucent. Add red peppers and continue cooking until softened.
Combine the cooked chicken pieces with the sautéed vegetables in a baking dish. Top with rinsed and drained rice.
Sprinkle fresh mint leaves over the top of the pilaf. Drizzle remaining olive oil evenly across the surface of the dish.
Bake for 25 minutes or until rice is tender and golden brown on all sides. Stir occasionally during cooking to prevent sticking.
Serve hot with a side of green salad.
